his_language: I never lose my head. Getting emotional about things doesn't solve anything.
what_expert_notices: Composure presented as maturity. The flat interior (poverty of affect/fear) dressed as admirable steadiness. Magnetic; the seam is the totality.

her_experience: His confidence is honestly the most attractive thing about him. Nothing seems to scare him or rattle him, and being around that makes me feel like everything's handled.
what_expert_notices: PURE enchantment, no suspicion. The fearlessness experienced as safety and magnetism. This is WHY she falls and stays. Cover that makes the danger believable.

what_expert_notices: Cobra heart-rate-drop humanized: calmer as threat rises, in a NON-relational moment (no anger at her). Reads as protective strength; the 'strange' is the faint seam she dismisses.

her_experience: There was one second where something passed over his face that I can't really describe, and then it was gone and he was laughing again. I'm sure it was nothing.
what_expert_notices: Glimpse-level only, no incident. A flicker of the cold interior, instantly repaired and doubted ('sure it was nothing'). Early-stage safe: nothing has happened she can narrate.

her_experience: Sometimes I catch him looking at me with this cool, almost evaluating expression, just for a beat, before the warm look comes back.
what_expert_notices: The instrumental gaze glimpsed: appraisal before warmth. Ties to observer cluster. A beat, not an event. She notes without naming.

his_language: Morning. What's your day looking like?
what_expert_notices: Pure presence, totally normal. Establishes the daily-contact baseline that later becomes an expectation. No tell in words; the tell is it's the first thread of a tether.

his_language: Just thinking about you. Send me a pic of where you're at.
what_expert_notices: Warm/flattering surface. Faint behavior-steering: normalizes reporting her location as romance, not monitoring. Glossable as 'he's so into me'.

his_language: You went quiet on me earlier. Everything okay?
what_expert_notices: Sounds caring. Installs the expectation of responsiveness: a gap now requires explanation. Seeds the first carefulness about reply speed. Up-position: her silence is now a thing she accounts for to him.

his_language: I'm a simple guy. If I text someone I care about, I just like to hear back. That's all respect really is to me.
what_expert_notices: Unarguable on its face (who's against respect?). Up-position: HE defines respectful responsiveness as the standard. Behavior-steering: she now monitors reply speed. Bancroft: her autonomy recoded as a respect issue. Young: Conditional Decency.

his_language: It's not about checking up on you. It's about respect. You respect someone, you don't leave them wondering.
what_expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it ('not checking up') — the denial is the tell. Recodes her ordinary unavailability as disrespect. Up-position: he sets the rule and frames it as a universal value.

his_language: I always get back to you right away. I just ask for the same.
what_expert_notices: Fake symmetry: sounds fair, but he authored the rule and enforces it from the up-position. The 'I just ask' makes a standing demand sound modest.

her_experience: He's so present. Texts me good morning every day, wants to know about my day. After guys who'd disappear for hours, having someone this attentive feels amazing.
what_expert_notices: Pure enchantment. The asymmetry/expectation invisible. Glossable as devotion; this is why she leans in.

her_experience: I've started keeping my phone on me and replying quicker. I just don't want him to feel like I'm ignoring him or that I don't care.
what_expert_notices: The behavior-modification, framed as her consideration. She's reorganizing around his expected contact. The trap closing, fully glossable.

her_experience: He's big on respect, and honestly he's right that leaving someone on read is kind of rude. So I've gotten better about replying quickly, I don't like the idea of him thinking I don't care.
what_expert_notices: STRONGEST tell: she's internalized his standard as her own moral growth ('he's right'), AND she's reading his moods ('never totally sure when... that quiet tone') — unpredictable, so she over-corrects. Fear-seeding + up-position fully installed. She calls it self-improvement. Distinguishes from Devin (nameable rule) by the mood-reading uncertainty.

his_language: I'm just a really passionate man. When I care about someone, I care all the way.
what_expert_notices: Lovely surface. Up-position: defines his intensity as a fixed admirable trait SHE accommodates ('that's just how I am'). Pre-frames future possessiveness as passion.

his_language: I'll be honest, I'm a jealous guy. It's only because I'm so crazy about you.
what_expert_notices: Confesses possessiveness as a charming flaw rooted in love. Behavior-steering: invites her to accept/manage his jealousy as the price of being loved this hard. Up-position: this is who I am, you adjust.

his_language: I love hard and I expect a lot, I won't apologize for that. Most people can't handle it.
what_expert_notices: Up-position + strong/weak: high expectations framed as depth most people are too weak for. Recruits her to prove she's not 'most people' by meeting them.

her_experience: He's the most passionate person I've ever been with. It's intense, way more than my exes, but I'd take that over someone who doesn't care at all.
what_expert_notices: Enchantment: intensity chosen over indifference. The 'expects a lot' accepted as the cost of passion. She's already accommodating, framed as preference.

his_language: I just want to make sure you're safe. Text me when you get in?
what_expert_notices: Warm, normal, friend-test passes easily. Behavior-steering: installs the check-in. Up-position: his peace of mind sets her routine. Protection framed; threat is the world, never him.

his_language: I'd never let anyone treat you the way your ex did. You're with a different kind of man now.
what_expert_notices: Positions himself as protector against other men + asserts the up-position ('different kind of man'). Recruits her past to elevate himself. Glossable as reassuring.

her_experience: He's really protective. He worries about me out on my own, wants me to check in. After feeling like nobody had my back, it's honestly a relief to feel looked after.
what_expert_notices: Enchantment: protection as relief. The dependence/monitoring invisible. The 'check in' she now does is behavior-modification she calls feeling cared for.

his_language: Some guy at work figured he could talk down to me. He figured out pretty quick that doesn't fly with me.
what_expert_notices: Conflict aimed OUTWARD (not at her) = early-safe. Strong/weak frame: disrespect met with putting-in-place. Previews the unpredictable temper and the respect-engine. Not admiration (vs Evan).

his_language: My ex was honestly a lot, completely impossible at the end. I'm just done being taken for granted by people I'd do anything for.
what_expert_notices: Ex-as-villain + non-accountability + the victim frame. Previews how he'll narrate her. 'Do anything for' sets up future debt.

his_language: I don't let people walk over me. Where I'm from, a man handles his business.
what_expert_notices: Strong/weak masculinity: manhood = not being subordinated. Strength/respect, not status. Reads as self-respect; the rigidity is the tell.

her_experience: He's had a lot of conflict with people, but when he explains it they really did wrong him. He won't be disrespected, which I respect. I have noticed I'm a little careful not to be one of the people who lets him down.
what_expert_notices: Believes his side (the hook), AND the fear-seed surfaces: she's 'a little careful not to let him down' — already self-positioning to avoid his disfavor. The conflict trail teaches her the stakes. Distinguishes from Devin: it's mood/disfavor (unpredictable), not a rule.

her_experience: He's so decisive, he just takes charge and handles everything. It's a relief after dating guys who couldn't make a decision.
what_expert_notices: Dominance experienced as welcome competence. Her decision-making quietly ceding; reframed as his strength. Cover that makes it work.

his_language: Don't worry about the bill, I've got it. I take care of my person, that's just how I am.
what_expert_notices: Provider-frame: generosity as the up-position ('I take care of', 'my person'). Minor for Marley (gifts lean Evan/display); here it's about HIS role as provider/leader, not showing off. Seeds debt.

his_language: (a beat where his tone goes flat and cool, then warm again) Sorry. I just hate the idea of losing you, you know how I am.
what_expert_notices: Glimpse-level only, NO incident. The cool flat beat (unpredictable) instantly repaired with love-language. Teaches her there's a line and she's not sure where it is = the fear-seed. Repair = love-as-reset.

her_experience: For a second his whole energy shifted and it caught me off guard, then he was sweet again right away. I felt silly for even reacting. He just feels things really strongly.
what_expert_notices: The flash startles, is repaired, doubted ('felt silly'), reframed as strong feeling. No narratable incident = early-safe. The 'caught off guard' is the unpredictability that seeds vigilance.

his_language: I'll drive you. I don't love the idea of you out on your own that late.
what_expert_notices: Directive about her movement costumed as protection. Imperative ('I'll drive you') softened by courtesy + concern. Layperson: chivalrous; expert: control of where she goes.

his_language: Text me when you get there so I know you're safe, okay?
what_expert_notices: Surveillance framed as care. The 'okay?' makes a monitoring requirement sound like a request. Establishes check-in expectation early.

his_language: Let me come with you, I don't like you dealing with that kind of thing alone.
what_expert_notices: Isolation-via-accompaniment: he attaches himself to her independent activities, framed as support. Erodes solo space.

his_language: Just let me handle it. You shouldn't have to deal with people like that.
what_expert_notices: Takes over her business framed as sparing her. Centralizes agency in him, courteously.

his_language: Why don't you call your friend tomorrow instead, stay in with me tonight. I've barely had you to myself.
what_expert_notices: Directs her social time, wrapped in romantic wanting. Isolation10 as devotion. 'Had you to myself' = ownership language, gentle.

her_experience: He's always offering to drive me, come with me, handle things for me. It's honestly kind of nice after guys who couldn't be bothered. He just likes taking care of me.
what_expert_notices: Residue: the directives experienced as welcome care. The pattern (he inserts himself into everything) invisible; reframed as attentiveness.

his_language: I didn't love how that guy was looking at you. I'm pretty protective of the people I care about.
what_expert_notices: Jealousy as protection; 'what's mine' = ownership stated plainly but warmly. The threat is framed as other men, never him.

her_experience: He's so protective of me. After guys who didn't care at all, having someone who wants to keep me safe and look out for me feels amazing.
what_expert_notices: Pure enchantment. Protection experienced as relief and love. The control it smuggles is invisible. Possessiveness as devotion (Bancroft).

her_experience: He gets a bit funny about other guys but only because he cares so much. He says he just can't stand the idea of anyone disrespecting me.
what_expert_notices: Residue: possessive jealousy reframed as protective care; note 'disrespecting me' = his respect-frame already installed in her account. Excused.

her_experience: He's had a lot of fallings-out with people, but honestly when he explains it they really did do him wrong. He just won't be disrespected, which I kind of admire.
what_expert_notices: Residue: she believes his side of every conflict, excuses the pattern, admires the 'strength'. Adopts his respect-frame. Never names the non-accountability.

her_experience: He talks about his exes like they were all crazy. I feel kind of special, like I'm finally the one who gets him and treats him right.
what_expert_notices: Residue: ex-as-villain flatters her as the exception. The danger (she'll be reclassified) invisible. Feeling-the-exception is the trap closing.

his_language: I give a lot in a relationship, I just hope for the same back.
what_expert_notices: Frames compliance as fairness/reciprocity. 'How this works' asserts his rules as natural law. Respect = mutual submission to his authority.

his_language: I'll admit I get a little jealous sometimes. It's only because I'm so into you.
what_expert_notices: Possessive jealousy as the measure of love. 'Can't stand the thought' = ownership; framed as the depth of his feeling. He likely means it (Intensity9), which disorients her.

his_language: I don't really do lukewarm. When I'm in, I'm all in.
what_expert_notices: Redefines love as his intense possessive version, framed as superior to normal relationships. Recruits her into believing the intensity IS love.

her_experience: He loves me so intensely, more than anyone ever has. I don't want to do anything that would hurt him or make him feel like I don't love him back.
what_expert_notices: Residue: passionately loved + already self-policing to protect his feeling. The love-as-compliance trap experienced as devotion. Never names it.

her_experience: He says I'm his and he's mine, that he's never felt this strongly about anyone. It's the most wanted I've ever felt.
what_expert_notices: Pure enchantment: ownership ('his and mine') experienced as being intensely wanted. The possession invisible under the intensity.

his_language: Don't worry about it, I've already taken care of it. I handle things.
what_expert_notices: Dominance10 as decisiveness. Reads as competence/strength after passive exes. Removes her from her own decisions, courteously.

his_language: I made us a reservation, figured you'd rather not have to think about it.
what_expert_notices: Takes over a shared decision framed as relieving her. Early it's attractive leadership; the not-consulting is the seam.

his_language: A man should lead. I take that seriously, taking care of my woman.
what_expert_notices: Traditional-masculinity framing of dominance. 'My woman' = ownership; 'lead' = his authority. Strong/weak (not admiration).

her_experience: He's so decisive and in control, he just handles everything. It's a relief honestly, I've always dated guys who couldn't make a decision.
what_expert_notices: Residue: dominance experienced as welcome competence and relief. The erosion of her decision-making invisible; reframed as his strength. Cover that makes it work.

his_language: I wouldn't call it jealous exactly, more protective. I just like looking after you.
what_expert_notices: Reframes jealousy as protection + ownership in one line. 'Mine to look after' fuses the protector frame and possession. Denies the jealousy while displaying it.
citation: Bancroft (possessiveness); Gottman Pit Bull [contested]; FIA dimensional Control

his_language: Saw that guy liked your photo. I won't lie, I noticed.
what_expert_notices: Early surveillance + control of her online space, possessive not panicked. Small and specific, easy to dismiss.
citation: Bancroft (possessiveness); Gottman Pit Bull [contested]; FIA dimensional Control

her_experience: He gets jealous but it's not insecure-jealous, it's more like he's so sure of us that he just doesn't want to share me. At first it was flattering.
what_expert_notices: Residue: ownership-jealousy experienced as flattering certainty (NOT the Clinger's neediness, which she'd find off-putting). 'At first' hints at coming unease without naming it.

his_language: I've never wanted anyone the way I want you. It's almost too much, I can't think straight.
what_expert_notices: Intensity9 as passion. Magnetic ('never wanted anyone like this'); the 'almost too much' is the faint seam of disproportion. Heat, not coldness (vs Morgan).
citation: FIA dimensional Intensity/Dysregulation; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

her_experience: It's the most passionate thing I've ever felt. The highs are incredible, even if the lows are rough. I've never felt so alive with someone.
what_expert_notices: Pure enchantment: the volatility experienced as passion and aliveness. The 'lows are rough' noted but subordinated to the highs. The disproportion not yet alarming.

her_experience: He feels everything so intensely. Sometimes his reactions are bigger than I'd expect but it's because he cares so deeply, more than anyone has.
what_expert_notices: Residue: disproportion ('bigger than I'd expect') registered and immediately excused as depth of caring. The seam she talks herself past.

her_experience: He got really intense over something small the other day, way more than it called for, and then it passed. I figured he was just stressed.
what_expert_notices: The disproportion seam: reaction too big for the trigger (Dysregulation8 + slight-as-disrespect). She attributes to stress, never to a pattern. Glimpse-level, no incident.
citation: FIA dimensional Dysregulation/Inconsistency; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

her_experience: There are a few topics he feels strongly about, so I tend to steer around them. Everyone has their things, it's not a big deal.
what_expert_notices: Residue: she's begun walking on eggshells ('being careful') and minimizing it ('not a big deal'). The hypervigilance forming. Never names it as control/fear.
citation: FIA dimensional Dysregulation/Inconsistency; Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

her_experience: For a second something flashed and it actually startled me, then he was sweet again and so loving, and I felt silly for reacting. He clearly just feels things strongly.
what_expert_notices: Residue: the flash startles her, is instantly repaired and doubted ('felt silly'), reframed as strong feeling. No incident she can narrate. Early-stage safe.

his_language: Order whatever you want. Might go easy tonight though, we've got an early start.
what_expert_notices: Permission-then-restriction: 'order whatever' immediately qualified. Controls her choice while sounding easygoing.

his_language: You could skip that one and stay in with me. I've barely had you to myself lately.
what_expert_notices: Discourages her independent social event by centering his comfort. Soft isolation.

his_language: My family doesn't get to talk to me however they want either. I cut my own brother off over it.
what_expert_notices: Conflict trail extends to family; total non-accountability. Reads as standards; the scorched-earth pattern is the tell.

his_language: I think when you're really with someone they kind of become your priority, you know?
what_expert_notices: Redefines commitment as isolation, framed as mutual priority. 'Before friends' is the isolating move dressed as devotion.

his_language: I'm a take-charge kind of guy. I like leading and looking after my person.
what_expert_notices: Disavows control while asserting it. The denial itself is the tell. Control reframed as partnership.

his_language: I just like us to feel like a team when we're around my friends.
what_expert_notices: Her independent behavior recoded as making him 'look bad' (disrespect to his standing). Small, early, easy to concede.

his_language: I think about you every second. It's intense, I know, but that's what real feeling is.
what_expert_notices: Intensity as proof of love; pre-empts 'too intense' by defining intensity AS real feeling. Heat, magnetic early.

her_experience: I've started checking in with him before I make plans. It just feels like the considerate thing to do when someone cares about your time as much as he does.
what_expert_notices: Compliance habit forming, minimized, with avoidance-of-his-reaction admitted. Eggshells. Never named.

her_experience: Everyone says how lucky I am to have someone so devoted and protective. I think they're right.
what_expert_notices: Social reinforcement of the protector frame; control reads to everyone as devotion. Pure, no suspicion.

her_experience: He can be a lot, but no one has ever loved me this hard. I'd rather have someone who feels everything than someone who feels nothing.
what_expert_notices: A lot' acknowledged then reframed as superior to indifference. She's chosen the intensity and rationalized the cost.

her_experience: When he tells me about the people who wronged him I really feel for him. He's been through a lot and people keep letting him down.
what_expert_notices: Believes his side of the conflict trail; the non-accountability invisible to her. Sympathy as the hook.

his_language: Wow. Okay. I just thought you'd want to spend the time with me, but no, it's fine.
what_expert_notices: Charlot warning sign: reacts negatively when she says no. Early = wounded cooling/sulk, NOT rage. 'It's fine' (clearly not fine) makes her decline cost something. Behavioral (declining an activity), not intellectual.
citation: Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign); Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement)

his_language: Yeah, no, do your thing. I'll just be here.
what_expert_notices: Passive-aggressive withdrawal in response to her 'no'. The chill is the consequence. Trains her that declining = managing his mood after.

her_experience: When I said I couldn't make it he got really quiet, I could tell he was let down. I hate disappointing him, he just wants to spend time with me, so I try to make most things work now.
what_expert_notices: The chain of two Charlot predictors: he reacts badly to no, so she stops saying no ('feels she can't say no'). Behavioral compliance via mood-consequence. The early coercion engine, fully glossable.

her_experience: I've noticed I go along with what he wants most of the time. It's not that he makes me, I guess I just like making him happy.
what_expert_notices: Charlot top predictor: 'feels she can't say no'. Her autonomy quietly ceding; 'it's not that he makes me' is the disavowal that hides the coercion. Pure structural tell.

his_language: You're too worked up to think about this clearly right now. Let me handle it.
what_expert_notices: Reframes her emotion as unfitness to ACT/decide, then takes the decision (behavioral authority). Distinct from Avery (who'd say she's WRONG) and Morgan (clinical 'dysregulated'). Marley = his right to decide. Water Torturer calm.
citation: Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement); Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign)

his_language: I'm staying calm here. One of us has to be the level-headed one.
what_expert_notices: Positions his calm as the authority to lead and her emotion as the problem. Bancroft Water Torturer: calm-as-weapon. Aimed at who decides/leads, not who's factually right.

his_language: When you get like this, that's exactly when you need to let me steer.
what_expert_notices: Her emotional state recoded as the trigger for him to take control of the situation/behavior. Compliance, not correctness. 'Steer' = behavioral authority.
citation: Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement); Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign)

his_language: We'll talk about it when you've settled down.
what_expert_notices: Sounds mature; functions to defer her independent position and make HER STATE the issue rather than his behavior. Dismisses without engaging. Water Torturer.

her_experience: He's so calm and steady when I get upset. He's good at talking me down, and usually by the end I feel a bit silly for getting so worked up about it.
what_expert_notices: The Water Torturer effect: his calm makes her the problem; outcome is his way (behavioral compliance) + her self-doubt about her own REACTIONS (not her facts/judgment, which would be Avery). She defers to avoid being 'the irrational one'.
citation: Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement); Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign)

his_language: You're not really going to keep talking to other guys now that we're a thing, right? ...kidding, kidding, no pressure.
what_expert_notices: Young Test-and-Apologize: probes a possessive boundary, then the 'kidding/no pressure' is the retractable cover. The test is real, the apology isn't. Glossable as a joke; gauges her tolerance for control.

his_language: Wear the red one tonight. ...your call obviously, I just like it.
what_expert_notices: Probes a directive over her appearance, then retracts ('your call obviously'). Tests whether a small instruction lands. Apology-escape-hatch = deniability.

her_experience: He says these slightly intense things and then immediately laughs them off or apologizes, so I never know if he means them. Probably just his sense of humor.
what_expert_notices: Young: the apology makes the boundary-probe gloss-able ('probably just humor'). She can't pin it down, which is the design. She doesn't name it as testing.

his_language: Just don't make a scene tonight, okay? For me. Let's keep it smooth.
what_expert_notices: Charlot-adjacent: pre-emptively polices her behavior/conduct ('don't make a scene'), framed as a small ask 'for me'. Behavioral compliance, not idea-control. Trains her to self-monitor in public.
citation: Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign); Bancroft (Water Torturer; entitlement)

her_experience: I want things to go well when we're out, especially around his friends, so I try to be on my best behavior. I just really want him to be happy.
what_expert_notices: The fear-engine destination, early: she monitors her own conduct to avoid an unpredictable reaction. Behavioral self-policing. 'Something small' = the disproportion she's learned to fear. Distinct from Devin (a known rule) by the unpredictability.
citation: Charlot et al. 2025 (predictive warning sign); Gottman Pit Bull [contested]

his_language: I'm not going to apologize for having standards. A man who folds every time isn't much of a man.
what_expert_notices: Marley's narrow version: not-backing-down as AUTHORITY/strength (strong-weak), NOT 'I'm intellectually right' (that's Avery). Kept minimal to protect the Avery boundary. Frames non-accountability as masculine resolve.
